raw_consumer_statement:
Paste this into * * What happened? * * : I am disputing any balance, interest, fees, collection activity, or negative reporting by Citibank/Citi Retail Services related to a Wayfair mattress purchase that arose from a failed merchant remedy after a Wayfair bed collapse.

This was not a normal voluntary purchase debt. My Wayfair bed collapsed around XX/XX/year> under XXXX # XXXX. Wayfair was aware that I needed a safe sleep setup because the collapse left me without a safe place to sleep and worsened my mobility/pain issues. I repeatedly told Wayfair that replacing only the bed frame would not solve the problem because the mattress was also damaged/unusable and I needed a complete safe-sleep accommodation.

Wayfair and/or its merchant/manufacturer partners later acknowledged the replacement mattress cost as reimbursable. Coaster confirmed reimbursement for a {$1500.00} replacement mattress, plus related assembly/removal costs. However, Wayfair then attempted to condition payment/reimbursement on signing a broad release that would affect my future legal rights, including injury-related claims. I refused to sign away future legal rights just to obtain reimbursement for a mattress that was needed because of the failed Wayfair bed. 

Because I needed safe sleep, I was effectively pressured into purchasing/replacing the mattress while the underlying merchant responsibility remained disputed. Citi is now treating the mattress-related balance as owed even though the charge is directly tied to Wayfairs failed remedy, unsafe-sleep situation, acknowledged reimbursement issue, and release-pressure conduct.

I am not asking Citi to decide my injury claim against Wayfair. I am asking Citi to properly investigate this as a disputed credit-card purchase and stop treating it as an ordinary debt. Citi should not collect, add fees/interest, or report the disputed amount as owed while ignoring the merchant evidence showing the mattress cost was part of the collapse-related remedy and reimbursement dispute. 

I want Citi to open or reopen a formal billing dispute, mark the amount as disputed, suspend collection activity, correct or delete any negative reporting tied to this charge, remove interest/fees tied to the disputed amount, and provide the full investigation file and merchant evidence Citi relied on to claim that I owe this balance. 

For the publish checkbox under the box : I would * * leave it unchecked * * unless you want this public later.
